    *   Components:
        *   Combustor body (general shape)
        *   114 mm distance annotation
        *   85 mm distance annotation
        *   CH4 inlet
        *   Air inlet
        *   Swirl generators (internal details not fully resolved)
        *   Reference height h = 0
        *   Fuel nozzle location in the inset

**5. Key numbers, constants, parameters:**

*   Thermal power: 10 kW
*   Thermoacoustic pulsation frequency: 295 Hz
*   Central nozzle diameter: 15 mm
*   Annular nozzle:
    *   Inner diameter (i.d.): 17 mm
    *   Outer diameter (o.d.): 25 mm, contoured to 40 mm
*   Plenum:
    *   Inner diameter 79 mm
    *   Height 65 mm
*   Radial swirlers:
    *   8 channels for central nozzle
    *   12 channels for annular nozzle
*   Air mass flow ratio (annular/central): 1.5
*   CH4 channels: 72 channels (0.5 mm x 0.5 mm)
*   CH4 slit width: < 0.5 mm
*   Exit plane distance (fuel/central air nozzles below outer air nozzle): 4.5 mm
*   Exhaust tube:
    *   Diameter 40 mm
    *   Length 50 mm

**Components and Labels (from Figure 2 Schematic):**

*   Nd:YAG laser
*   Dye laser 283 nm
*   PIV
*   McKenna burner

*   Combustion chamber
*   Beam dump
*   Bandpass filter (295-340 nm)

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Combustion chamber dimensions: 85 x 85 mm² cross-section, 114 mm height
*   Quartz window rod diameter: 10 mm
*   Equivalence ratio (φ): 0.75
*   Thermal power (Pth): 10.3 kW
*   CH4 mass flow: 12.3 g/min
*   Air mass flow: 280.8 g/min
*   Thermo-acoustic oscillation frequency: 295 Hz
*   Microphones: Brüel & Kjaer 4939
*   Microphone height: 16mm
*   Nozzle diameter: 25 mm
*   Nozzle Reynolds number: 15000
* Swirl number (S): 0.55
*   OH-PLIF Dye Laser: 283 nm
*   PIV Laser: 532 nm
*   UV beam (pulse duration: 8 ns, line width 0.4 cm-1)
*   Bandpass filter: 295-340 nm

*   Pulse energy: 2.5 mJ
*   OH transition: Q1(8)
*   Wavelength: 283 nm (OH), 310 nm, 295-340 nm
*   Temperature range: 1400-2200 K
*   Laser sheet height: 50 mm
*   Laser sheet thickness: 400 µm
*   Laser intensity: 3.1 MW/cm² /cm⁻¹, 1 MW/cm² /cm⁻¹
*   OH-PLIF and dye cell fluorescence camera exposure time: 400 ns
*   Image intensifier exposure time: 40µs
*   Spatial resolution: 185 µm
*   PIV Laser pulse energy: 120 mJ/pulse.
*   PIV Laser wavelength: 532 nm
*   Vertical sheet height: 60 mm
*   Thickness laser sheet: 1 mm
*   Time separation between laser pulses: 15 µs
*   Seeding particle diameter: 1 µm
*   Velocity resolution: 0.77 mm
*   Image acquisition rate: 2.5 double frames/s
*   Interrogation window size: 64x64 pixel to 16x16 pixel
*   OH -PLIF and PIV cameras framing at 2.5 Hz, laser system operating at 10 Hz
*   Time delay after first PIV Laser pulse: 10µs
*   Temperature lower bound for OH abundance: 1400K
*   Timescale for two-body flames: 20µs
*   Timescale for three-body flames: 3ms
*   CCD camera (Roper Scientific, 512x512 pixels).
*   CCD camera (LaVision, 1376 x 1024 pixels)
*   Achromatic UV lens (Halle, f/2, f = 64 mm)
*   Nikon lens (f/2.8, f = 50 mm)
*   Nominal diameter: 1 µm

title: 08_simultanious_OH-plif_and_PIV_measurement_in_gas_turbine_combustor_model
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 6]
Here is the technical content from the provided gas turbine research page:

1.  **Mathematical equations:**
     - NO EQUATIONS

2.  **Graphs/charts:**
    *   Figure 5a: Time averaged OH-PLIF distribution of the flame
        *   Axes: r (mm) from -40 to 40, h (mm) from 0 to 60
        *   Plots: Heatmap of OH-PLIF distribution

    *   Figure 5b: Instantaneous OH-PLIF distribution of the flame
        *   Axes: r (mm) from -40 to 40, h (mm) from 0 to 60
        *   Plots: Heatmap of OH-PLIF distribution

3.  **Tables:**
    *   NO TABLES

4.  **Diagrams:**
    *   Figure 5: a Time averaged b instantaneous OH-PLIF distribution of the flame

5.  **Key numbers, constants, parameters:**
    *   h ≈ 30 mm (heat release zone height)
    *   Flame stabilizes at 2-5 mm above fuel nozzle exit
    *   Absorption ≈ 44% (OH radicals)
    *   r = -40 to 0 mm
    *   Mixture temperature less than ≈ 1400 K
    *   Temperature in the region around 1950 K.
    *   r = -10 to 10 mm, h > 15 mm
    *   h = 5-30 mm, r = -25 to 0 mm
    *   Φ ≈ 1
    *   Flame temperature ≈ 2200 K
    *   GRI 3.0 mechanism
    *   Φ = 0.75, 1.0
    *   T = 300 K
    *   Temperature range from 750-1500 K.
    *   Φ = 0.75, 1.0
    *   Burnt gas at 300 K
    *   Resulting temperatures were 750, 1000, 1250, 1500 K
    *   Φ = 1.0, maximum OH mole fraction ≈ 0.0075
    *   Φ = 0.75, maximum OH mole fraction ≈ 0.005. equilibrium is ≈ 0.001

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   phi_global = 0.75

**2. Graphs:**

*   **Figure 6: Dependence of superequilibrium OH concentration on different temperature and equivalence ratio combinations**
    *   **Axes:**
        *   x-axis: Time / s
        *   y-axis: OH Mole Fraction
    *   **Curves:**
        *   phi = 1; T = 300 K
        *   phi = 1; T = 750 K
        *   phi = 1; T = 1250 K
        *   phi = 0.75; T = 300 K
        *   phi = 0.75; T = 750 K
        *   phi = 0.75; T = 1250 K

**3. Tables:**
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Figure 7: Approximate spatial position and structure of the reaction zone deduced from the OH-PLIF distribution in Fig. 5b. Inset: zoomed in views of regions marked A and B**
    *  Axes:
             *   x-axis: r (mm)
             *   y-axis: h (mm)
    * Components: reaction zone regions marked A and B
*   **Figure 8: Two examples of instantaneous CH-PLIF images**
    *  Axes:
             *   x-axis: r (mm)
             *   y-axis: h (mm)

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   phi_global = 0.75
*   OH mole fraction close to 0.001 (calculated)
*  r = -30 to 40 mm
*   Axial velocity: approximately 15 m/s
*   Distance traveled by superequilibrium OH: approximately 15 mm in 1 ms
*   Threshold Value: 5% of the maximum value (for masking gradients)
